input.prompt: Photorealistic underwater wildlife photograph of a Balaena mysticetus bowhead whale, the rarely-photographed species famous for its enormous triangular arched bow-shaped head taking up ONE THIRD of its body length. The mouth-line curves dramatically upward. The body is stocky compact and rotund. Completely smooth dark slate-blue back, NO dorsal fin, NO hump, NO long flippers. Short paddle-shaped pectoral flippers. Bright white chin patch with mottled grey speckled markings. Cold Arctic ocean water, filtered sunlight from above. National Geographic wildlife photography style, sharp detail, marine biology textbook accuracy.
